name: distilabel
tagline: Build synthetic-data and AI-feedback pipelines that run on any LLM
seoTitle: distilabel: Synthetic Data & AI Feedback Pipelines
metaDescription: distilabel is an open-source Python framework for building synthetic-data and AI-feedback pipelines that call any LLM to generate, label, and judge datasets.
overview: distilabel is a Python framework for generating synthetic data and AI feedback. You write pipelines in code that call large language models to create new examples, label existing ones, and judge their quality, then export the result as a dataset you can use for fine-tuning or evaluation.

It is aimed at engineers and researchers who need to build training and preference datasets without labeling everything by hand. It works for traditional NLP tasks like classification and extraction, as well as generative tasks like instruction following, dialogue, and LLM-as-a-judge scoring.

As a synthetic-data and labeling tool, distilabel sits between your raw inputs and your model training. It uses one unified API across many LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Hugging Face, Ollama, vLLM, and others), so you can swap models without rewriting your pipeline.
features: Pipeline API that chains steps and tasks such as TextGeneration to produce and process data
One unified interface to many LLM backends, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Cohere, Groq, Ollama, vLLM, and Hugging Face Inference Endpoints
Built-in tasks for synthetic data generation, labeling, and AI feedback (LLM-as-a-judge)
Optional extras let you install only the LLM integrations you need (for example, hf-inference-endpoints, openai, vllm)
Integrates with the Hugging Face datasets ecosystem for loading inputs and pushing results to the Hub
Designed for scalable pipelines with fault tolerance, based on published research methods

gettingStarted.intro: Install distilabel with the LLM integration you want, then define a Pipeline and run it over a dataset. The example below uses Hugging Face Inference Endpoints.

steps.heading: Install distilabel
steps.body: Requires Python 3.9 or newer. Add an extra in brackets to pull in a specific LLM integration, such as hf-inference-endpoints.

steps.heading: Define and run a pipeline
steps.body: Wrap your steps in a Pipeline context, attach an LLM to a task like TextGeneration, then call pipeline.run on a dataset and push the result to the Hub.
steps.code:
from datasets import load_dataset

from distilabel.models import InferenceEndpointsLLM
from distilabel.pipeline import Pipeline
from distilabel.steps.tasks import TextGeneration

with Pipeline() as pipeline:
    TextGeneration(
        llm=InferenceEndpointsLLM(
            model_id="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
            generation_kwargs={"temperature": 0.7, "max_new_tokens": 512},
        ),
    )

if __name__ == "__main__":
    dataset = load_dataset("distilabel-internal-testing/instructions", split="test")
    distiset = pipeline.run(dataset=dataset)
    distiset.push_to_hub(repo_id="distilabel-example")

useCases: Generate synthetic instruction or dialogue datasets to fine-tune your own LLM
Build preference (DPO) datasets by having an LLM judge and rank candidate responses
Label or filter existing data at scale using AI feedback instead of manual annotation
Create task-specific evaluation sets that follow methods from recent research papers
